title: No TOFU (Trust On First Use) or certificate pinning for server identity
description: The ECDH key exchange did not implement any form of server identity verification or certificate pinning, making the protocol vulnerable to MITM attacks.
recommendation: Implement TOFU: on first connection, the client should store the server's public key fingerprint and warn/reject on subsequent connections if the key changes.

patch.summary: Implemented full TOFU (Trust On First Use) via persistent server identity key (P7 v1.3)
details: P7 v1.3: new fields server_identity_key (id=19), server_identity_sig (id=20), strict_identity (id=21) in server_key message
ServerIdentity class: persists P256 keypair in wired-identity.key; signs ECDH public key per connection
ServerIdentityProvider protocol on P7Socket; identityProvider set by ServerController
serverTrustHandler closure on P7Socket for client-side TOFU decision (app-level callback)
Server signs ephemeral ECDH public key with persistent identity key in acceptKeyExchange
Client verifies identity signature and calls serverTrustHandler in connectKeyExchange
strict_identity = yes in config.ini (default); clients must hard-fail on key change
WiredServerViewModel: identityFingerprint display, strictIdentity toggle, exportIdentityKey() with NSSavePanel
